summary:
To improve retention and ensure tight fit, a bevel creates enhanced surface contacts that prevent gaps between the restoration and natural teeth, crucially aiding bacterial exclusion while securing the dental work effectively into place. Agent1 reasoned that the primary function of adding a bevel during fabrication of an inlay or 3/4 crown involves increasing its ability to stay securely attached inside the mouth without leaving spaces where harmful microorganisms could enter; this directly supports maintaining oral health as well as structural support over time. \boxed{Enhancing retention and sealing margins are central goals achieved through strategic use of bevelling techniques}. 

This summary captures both core aspects highlighted earlier—retention improvement due to tighter fitting around prepared surfaces—and also touches upon how such precision contributes towards long-term success rates concerning durability alongside infection prevention measures taken at each stage post-procedure completion.
